content: First-line: non-pharmacological interventions—identify/treat underlying medical causes (pain, infection, constipation), optimize environment (reduce noise, maintain routines), address unmet needs, person-centered care, meaningful activities, music therapy, bright light therapy for sleep. Caregiver education/support essential. Pharmacotherapy reserved for severe symptoms causing danger/distress: antidepressants (SSRIs like citalopram, sertraline) for depression/anxiety; cholinesterase inhibitors/memantine may help some behavioral symptoms. Antipsychotics (risperidone, olanzapine, quetiapine) only for severe agitation/psychosis unresponsive to other measures due to increased mortality/stroke risk in elderly dementia patients (black box warning); use lowest dose, shortest duration, regular reassessment for discontinuation.
